checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?
Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill end=2014-02-01, value=85135406.28 / Goodwill end=2014-02-01, value=2424216.45 (down from 5552912.95)
evidence.location: 10-Q balance sheet Q1 FY2015 (comparative FY2014 year-end)

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?
Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: GrossProfit start=2013-02-03 end=2014-02-01, value=121890343.98 (margin ~33.2%, stable vs FY2013 32.7%) despite inventory buildup amid revenue decline

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?
Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent end=2012-01-28, value=11171845.73 vs AccountsReceivableNetCurrent end=2012-01-28, value=58597844.5 (ratio ~19.1%)
AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent end=2014-05-03, value=8891745.77 vs AccountsReceivableNetCurrent end=2014-05-03, value=73727257.81 (ratio ~12.1%)

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: No NT 10-K, NT 10-Q, 10-K/A, or 10-Q/A filings appear in the chronology between 2009 and the 2014-06-11 cutoff; most recent NT 10-K was filed 2008-04-16, well before the financial data window analyzed.

Multiple CORRESP/UPLOAD filings in 2010 (2010-06-30, 2010-07-22, 2010-07-23, 2010-08-17) and 2013 (2013-02-01, 2013-02-22) indicate SEC comment-letter correspondence, a routine review process rather than an enforcement flag.

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Financial series provide quarterly and annual data points from FY2010 (period ended 2010-01-30) through Q1 FY2015 (period ended 2014-05-03), spanning ~13 quarterly/annual filings across balance sheet, income statement, and cash flow concepts.
AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent data only begins at 2012-01-28, limiting pre-2012 reserve-trend analysis.

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Reduction of the allowance for doubtful accounts (and its coverage ratio relative to gross receivables) while accounts receivable balances grew to a multi-year high, implying understated bad-debt expense/reserve, which would overstate net receivables and net income.

rationale_evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent end=2012-01-28, value=11171845.73 vs AccountsReceivableNetCurrent end=2012-01-28, value=58597844.5 (ratio ~19.1%)
AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent end=2014-05-03, value=8891745.77 vs AccountsReceivableNetCurrent end=2014-05-03, value=73727257.81 (ratio ~12.1%)

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Receivables grew ~19.9% (2012-01-28 to 2013-02-02) while revenue actually declined ~1.1% over the same comparative period, consistent with extended credit terms, channel stuffing, or delayed collections inflating reported receivables/revenue recognition timing; partially reversed the following year.

Inventory increased ~12.8% (2013-02-02 to 2014-02-01) despite a ~5.9% revenue decline over the same period, while gross margin remained essentially flat (~32.7% to ~33.2%); this pattern is consistent with insufficient markdown/obsolescence reserves against slower-moving inventory rather than the margin deterioration one would expect if inventory were being appropriately written down.

top_signals: Allowance for doubtful accounts coverage ratio fell from ~19% (FY2012) to ~12% (Q1 FY2015) even as accounts receivable hit a multi-year high, suggesting under-reserving that could inflate net receivables and earnings.
Accounts receivable grew ~19.9% in FY2013 while revenue declined ~1.1% over the same period, a divergence later partially reversed.

Large net income vs. operating cash flow gap in FY2012 (NI=$10.27M vs OCF=$0.29M) and a swing to net loss with near-zero OCF in FY2014 (NI=-$9.17M vs OCF=$0.09M), indicating earnings quality concerns tied to working-capital/non-cash items.
Inventory grew ~12.8% in FY2014 while revenue fell ~5.9%, with gross margin holding flat, raising obsolescence/reserve-adequacy questions.

A sudden, large goodwill and intangible asset write-down occurred in Q4 FY2014 (Goodwill $5.55M→$2.42M; Intangibles $99.02M→$85.14M) coincident with an $11.37M quarterly net loss, consistent with correction of previously overstated soft assets.
No late filings, NT forms, or restatements (10-K/A, 10-Q/A) appear within the FY2010–2014 window covered by the financial data, reducing (but not eliminating) procedural red flags.
